name: Mimeophon
description: Stereo multi-zone audio repeater co-designed with Tom Erbe. Eight nested Zones span 1.3ms Karplus-Strong to 41s looping. Skew detunes L/R, Halo smears stereo, Flip reverses, Color shapes feedback timbre, Tempo input syncs to clock.

controls.name: Mix
controls.description: Combo pot. Unpatched: sets balance between dry live input and wet Repeats. With signal at Mix CV IN: acts as attenuator for that CV.
controls.detail: CCW: dry · CW: wet · 0–8V CV range

controls.name: Repeats
controls.description: Sets number of echo repeats from 1 to infinite. Extreme CW self-oscillates. While HOLD is engaged, this knob instead sets the starting point of the held repeat.

controls.name: Zone
controls.description: Eight-position stepped selector. Each Zone is a delay-time range, nested inside the next. Switching Zones crossfades without doppler, allowing instantaneous size changes.

controls.name: Rate
controls.description: Continuous frequency of Repeats within the selected Zone. When Skew is off, modulates both channels with doppler pitch-shift. When clocked via TEMPO, uses slew-less crossfade to jump between multiples without doppler.
controls.detail: CCW: slower · CW: faster · with TEMPO: x2 to /2 in fixed ratios

controls.name: Color
controls.description: Timbre of the feedback path. CCW darker (oil-can / BBD), noon crunchy, CW crispy digital. Color also shapes Halo. Cumulative over successive repeats — modulation speed changes timbral drift.

controls.name: Halo
controls.description: Adds stereo width and density around each repeat. Higher values inject smeared ambient excess into the feedback network, feeding Color.

controls.name: SKEW Button
controls.description: Press to engage Skew — Rate now controls L and R repeat frequencies in opposite directions, creating stereo phase-shifted patterns. Hold to toggle Ping Pong (channels not skewed) or Swap (channels skewed).
controls.detail: Press = skew · hold = Ping Pong / Swap

controls.name: HOLD Button
controls.description: Freezes the feedback buffer — no new sound enters or leaves. All other controls remain active for non-destructive modulation of the held repeat. Repeats knob becomes start-point while held.

controls.name: FLIP Button
controls.description: Plays the buffer backwards. In Zones 2–7 = reverse audio. In Zones 0–1 = 'Total Protonic Reversal' distortion (audio-rate buffer flipping every 70 samples).

inputs.name: L (Mono) Input
inputs.description: Left/mono audio input. Modular level, typically 10Vpp.

inputs.name: R Input
inputs.description: Right audio input. Normalized to L when unpatched — mono signals feed both channels automatically.

inputs.name: Zone CV Input
inputs.description: CV for Zone selection, through the attenuverter.

inputs.name: Rate CV Input
inputs.description: CV for Rate, through the attenuverter.

inputs.name: microRate Input
inputs.description: Dedicated un-attenuated doppler-modulation destination. Continuous, consistent range across all Zones. Linear in Zones 1–7; 1V/oct exponential in Zone 0 (useful for Karplus-Strong tone).

inputs.name: TEMPO Input
inputs.description: External clock in. Maps Rate to nearest musical division/multiple per Zone; switches use slew-less crossfade (no doppler).

inputs.name: FLIP Input
inputs.description: Gate toggles FLIP on/off. Audio-rate modulation in small Zones creates interesting sounds.

outputs.name: RATE Output
outputs.description: Trigger output at the current Rate (both channels OR'ed when Skew is engaged). Usable as clock divider/multiplier when externally clocked. Stays HIGH at fastest Rates (Zone 0 / fast Zone 1).

behaviors.name: Nested Zones
behaviors.description: Zones are nested from shortest to longest — Z1 contains Z0, Z2 contains Z1, etc. Incoming audio always writes into all Zones simultaneously. Switching from a small Zone to a larger one can unearth sounds from seconds or minutes ago.

behaviors.name: Zone 0 Karplus-Strong
behaviors.description: Zone 0 (1.3–20.4ms) into self-oscillation produces string-like physical-modeling tone. microRate tracks 1V/oct, giving pitched plucked-string voices. Short input bursts (noise, impulses, transients) 'excite' the string; Color/Halo shape decay.

behaviors.name: Skew
behaviors.description: Rate controls L and R repeat frequencies in opposite directions, producing stereo phase-shifted echo patterns. In smallest two Zones one channel may bottom out before the knob hits minimum. Toggle on/off while Rate is still to resync L/R.

behaviors.name: Ping Pong / Swap
behaviors.description: If channels not skewed: engages Ping Pong — L+R inputs sum to mono and repeats bounce between L/R outputs. If channels are skewed: engages Swap — L/R feedback paths exchange each repeat, producing phase-shifted stereo pairs. Zone Window flashes to indicate.

behaviors.name: HOLD preserves buffer
behaviors.description: Freezes the feedback loop non-destructively. Color, Halo, Rate, Zone, Skew, Flip, Mix all remain active for endless manipulation of a captured phrase. Repeats knob becomes the start-point of the held repeat for current Zone.

behaviors.name: FLIP + small-Zone protonic reversal
behaviors.description: In Zones 2–7, FLIP cleanly reverses audio. In Zones 0–1, the buffer flips every 70 samples — 'Total Protonic Reversal' — generating harmonic/subharmonic distortion derived from input spectrum.

behaviors.name: TEMPO doppler-free rate
behaviors.description: External clock maps Rate to 12 fixed ratios (x2 full CCW through 1/1 at 12:30 to /2 full CW). Rate changes become slew-less crossfades — no doppler pitch-shift on transitions. Accepts 0.25Hz–50Hz; tap tempo works via two pulses.

behaviors.name: microRate doppler
behaviors.description: Dedicated audio-rate doppler modulation, independent of TEMPO sync state. Produces chorus, vibrato, flange. Skew inverts µ response on the right channel — great for stereo chorus with a slow LFO.

behaviors.name: 4-step input gain (mp82+)
behaviors.description: Cycles through −3dB Blue, 0dB Green (Unity), +3dB Orange, +6dB Red. Zone Window displays current setting. mp82 saves the selection across power cycles.

behaviors.name: Clear all Zones (mp70+)
behaviors.description: Simultaneously setting Mix and Repeats fully CCW clears every Zone's buffer instantly — useful for resetting stuck feedback or removing latent content before a new section.

patchIdeas.name: Karplus-Strong string
patchIdeas.patch: Zone 0 · Repeats ~1:00 · Halo CCW · Mix 1:00 · Color 12:00 · trigger a MATHS pluck (Rise CCW, Fall 10–12, Response 2:00) into L input · pitch CV → µRate for 1V/oct · plays a plucked-string voice
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIMEOPHON:
* Zone: 0
* Repeats: 1:00
* Halo: full CCW
* Color: 12:00
* Mix: 1:00

- MATHS (CH1 Out) -> Mimeophon (L In) // short burst excites string
- Keys (Pitch) p> Mimeophon (microRate) // 1V/oct in Zone 0
- Mimeophon (L Out) -> Output (L)
- Mimeophon (R Out) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Tempo-synced dub delay
patchIdeas.patch: Zone 4–5 · Color 3:00 · Mix 11:00 · Repeats 12:00 · Halo to taste · system clock → TEMPO · engage Skew for stereo polyrhythmic echoes
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIMEOPHON:
* Zone: 4
* Color: 3:00
* Mix: 11:00
* Repeats: 12:00
* SKEW: ON

- Source (Out) -> Mimeophon (L In)
- Clock (Out) c> Mimeophon (TEMPO In)
- Mimeophon (L Out) -> Output (L)
- Mimeophon (R Out) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo chorus
patchIdeas.patch: Color 3:00 · Repeats ~12:00 · Mix 1:00 · slow ±LFO (≤10Hz) attenuated → µRate · SKEW on · Zone 1 for flange, Zone 0–1 for shimmer
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIMEOPHON:
* Zone: 0 (or 1 for flange)
* Color: 3:00
* Mix: 1:00
* SKEW: ON

- Source (Out) -> Mimeophon (L In)
- LFO (Tri, ≤10Hz) >> Mimeophon (microRate) // attenuated
- Mimeophon (L Out) -> Output (L)
- Mimeophon (R Out) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Back and forth to the future
patchIdeas.patch: Rate Out → FLIP In · Zone 3–5 · Mix 12:00 · Repeats ~9:00 · HOLD off · repeats alternate forward/backward automatically · patch clock to TEMPO for stricter rhythm
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIMEOPHON:
* Zone: 4
* Mix: 12:00
* Repeats: 9:00

- Source (Out) -> Mimeophon (L In)
- Mimeophon (RATE Out) g> Mimeophon (FLIP In) // auto forward/reverse
- Mimeophon (L Out) -> Output (L)
- Mimeophon (R Out) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Zone-sequenced rhythmic lattice
patchIdeas.patch: Sequence Zone at same rate as a melody in Zone 0 · smaller-Zone repeats get preserved in larger Zones · Repeats up builds a rhythmic echo lattice of different repeat styles per note
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIMEOPHON:
* Repeats: 2:00
* Mix: 12:00

- VCO (Out) -> Mimeophon (L In)
- Sequencer (CV) >> Mimeophon (Zone CV) // per-step zone
- Clock (Out) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- Mimeophon (L Out) -> Output (L)
- Mimeophon (R Out) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Held drone scrubber
patchIdeas.patch: Record a phrase · HOLD gate high · modulate Repeats (start-point), Color, Halo, Zone · infinite non-destructive scrubbing through a frozen moment
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIMEOPHON:
* HOLD: engaged

- Source (Out) -> Mimeophon (L In)
- Gate (Out) g> Mimeophon (HOLD In) // freeze buffer
- LFO1 (Out) >> Mimeophon (Repeats CV) // scan start-point
- LFO2 (Out) >> Mimeophon (Color CV)
- Mimeophon (L Out) -> Output (L)
- Mimeophon (R Out) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Filter-verb
patchIdeas.patch: Mix full CW · Repeats + Rate full CCW · Zone 0 · access Color and Halo with no audible repeats · FLIP for protonic reversal harmonics · Repeats up to taste for trails
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIMEOPHON:
* Zone: 0
* Mix: full CW
* Repeats: full CCW
* Rate: full CCW

- Source (Out) -> Mimeophon (L In)
- Mimeophon (L Out) -> Output (L)
- Mimeophon (R Out) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Self-oscillation drone
patchIdeas.patch: Nothing in inputs · Mix full CW · Zone 0 · Repeats full CW · slowly modulate Color (Wogglebug) and Zone (stepped random) · Rate Out → TEMPO In to cancel doppler on rate changes
patchIdeas.diagram:
MIMEOPHON:
* Zone: 0
* Mix: full CW
* Repeats: full CW
* Halo: CCW

- Wogglebug (Woggle CV) >> Mimeophon (Color CV)
- Random (Stepped) >> Mimeophon (Zone CV)
- Mimeophon (RATE Out) c> Mimeophon (TEMPO In) // cancel doppler
- Mimeophon (L Out) -> Output (L)
- Mimeophon (R Out) -> Output (R)
